content.problemStatement:
Indian Railways plans to optimize their platform management by simulating train arrivals and departures at their busiest station.

You are given two arrays ATATAT (Arrival Times) and DTDTDT (Departure Times) of length nnn, where AT[i]AT[i]AT[i] and DT[i]DT[i]DT[i] represent the arrival and departure times of the ithi^{th}ith train. Your task is to find the minimum number of platforms needed at the railway station to accommodate all trains such that no train has to wait for a platform.

**Note:**


Each train arrives and departs on the same day (e.g., DT[i]>AT[i]DT[i] > AT[i]DT[i]>AT[i] for all iii).
Times are given in 24-hour format without colons (e.g., 905905905 for 9:05 AM, 211021102110 for 9:10 PM).
Times have no leading zeros (e.g., 101010 for 12:10 AM, not 001000100010).
Multiple trains can arrive or depart at the exact same time.
Function DeclarationFunction NamefindMinPlatformsfindMinPlatformsfindMinPlatforms – This function computes the minimum number of platforms required at the station so that no train is kept waiting.

Parameters
ATATAT : A list/array of integers of length NNN, representing the arrival times of the trains.
DTDTDT : A list/array of integers of length NNN, representing the corresponding departure times of the trains.
nnn : An integer representing the total number of trains.
Return ValueReturns an integer: The minimum number of platforms needed to safely accommodate all trains without overlapping conflicts.
constraints: 0≤AT[i]<DT[i]≤23590 \leq AT[i] < DT[i] \leq 23590≤AT[i]<DT[i]≤2359 for each 0≤i<n0 \leq i < n0≤i<n
Both ATATAT and DTDTDT represent valid times in a 24-hour clock system.
content.outputFormat:
Input Format

First line contains a single integer TTT  - number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains a single integer nnn - number of trains
Second line contains nnn space-separated integers AT[i] - arrival times
Third line contains nnn space-separated integers DT[i] - departure times




Output Format

For each test case, print a single line containing the minimum number of platforms required

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1:
Trains timeline:

Train 1: 900 - 910
Train 2: 940 - 1200
Train 3: 950 - 1120
Train 4: 1100 - 1130
Train 5: 1500 - 1900
Train 6: 1800 - 2000

Maximum overlap occurs at 950 when Train 1, Train 2, and Train 3 need platforms simultaneously.
